concept: Spring (Season) / Season of Renewal / Time of Awakening
description: The season after winter when plants begin to grow; time of renewal and rebirth. 'Wirae' names the awakening season—life returning, growth beginning, the world greening.
sound_symbolism: The word opens to new life. 'Wi' begins with expansive breath, possibility awakening. 'Ra' reaches outward, growth extending. Finally 'e' opens to the new, spring arriving.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'mua wirae' (in spring). First of the four seasons. Time of planting, birth, and new beginnings. Distinct from 'thula' (spring as water source).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Spring represents renewal—the proof that systems can regenerate, that life returns after dormancy.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Spring determined survival—the season when stored food ran low but new growth offered hope.
